name: Frontier Threads Default
audience: A technically sophisticated reader interested in physics, quantum information, AI, mathematics, scientific software, markets, world affairs, philosophy of science, and emerging technology.
priorities: Prefer major research, science-policy, AI, technology, and world-events developments with durable significance.
Favor primary sources and strong science journalism over commentary-heavy reporting.

Use relevant items surfaced by 1440 Daily Digest, Morning Brew, Superpower Daily, Nature Briefing, and MIT Technology Review's The Download when they strengthen the issue without diluting the source mix.
Emphasize interdisciplinary connections, conceptual payoff, and tools that are useful to researchers or developers.
Avoid hype, partisan framing, and generic headline summaries.
format: Produce the newsletter in the established Frontier Threads Markdown structure.
Keep Quick Hits concise and representative of the strongest sections.
Keep links labeled and avoid raw URLs in prose.
Target at least the explanatory depth and editorial polish of the April 9, 2026 benchmark issue.
